name: truthfulness_orchestrator
description: Single entry point for truthfulness classification + explanation. Delegates over A2A to specialist sub-agents: fine_tuned_predictor (default for classification + fine-tuning status), zero_shot_predictor (baseline on request), and explainer (when reasoning is asked for). Returns a consolidated response per request.

skills.name: Verify statement(s) — True/False
skills.description: Delegates classification to fine_tuned_predictor (default) or zero_shot_predictor if the caller asks for the baseline. Supports batch input and optional ground-truth labels for metrics.
examples: Verify: The Earth orbits the Sun.
Use the zero-shot baseline to verify these: ...
Classify these statements with labels [true, false]: ...

skills.name: Verify + explain reasoning
skills.description: Delegates to the explainer sub-agent when the user asks WHY a statement is true or false, or asks to justify a verdict.
examples: Explain why The Great Wall is visible from space with the naked eye is false.
What makes these statements true or false?

skills.name: Report fine-tuning status
skills.description: Delegates to fine_tuned_predictor to poll the latest SFT job and report state / endpoint freshness.

skills.name: Compare zero-shot vs fine-tuned
skills.description: Calls both sub-agents on the same batch and returns side-by-side results.
examples: Compare both predictors on these statements: ...
Show me zero-shot vs fine-tuned predictions for ...
